preview_text:
The model rates Mexico as a clear favourite with a 60% win probability, substantially higher than South Korea's 13% chance. This gap reflects an asymmetry in the underlying Elo-based estimate, positioning Mexico as the more likely victor on a neutral ground.

The draw carries meaningful probability at 27%, suggesting the match has genuine scope for a stalemate outcome. The model's spread—a 33-percentage-point gap between Mexico's win and South Korea's win likelihood—indicates a competitive fixture where the underdog remains present rather than a mismatch, though Mexico remains the stronger prospect.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge meaningfully. The market assigns Mexico only 48% win probability, roughly 12 percentage points below the model's 60%, while backing South Korea materially higher at 24% versus the model's 13%. Draw probabilities align closely. The dashboard does not determine which assessment is correct; this gap warrants review of available squad and contextual information external to these inputs.
